Tq q-{ sr at rcal rmrft-fr arrr qtf,Sr sc t ffi Tm h ftT erF_+,r_r+ *: q-'{ fr ffi qpo dr rq w-qr-s * ?dBtT{ il{ Tfi ear+r a-ft c-rr.Tr s-r E-+,-ft:-t tq ET} mr gk,go 3tzfr1 d ft-+r rnrr 6r lsr.q. 1 201 5 t 1 j t2oj7 _fifr-il (risil)l fi.q"r. m-fl-, sTtr t-fu{ }IINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND E}tPOWER]VIENT I'iOTIFIC.4,TION New Delhi. the 23rd Augusr, 20lg G.S.R.800(E)._ln exercise of the powers conf.erred by clause (2.1 of articte J3gB of the Constitution antl in supersession of the National commission fbr Backward classes (Salaries and Allowances and other conditions of service ofchairperson and Members) Rules, 1996, except as respects things done or omitted to be done before such supersession, President hereby makes the fb[owing rules, namely:_ l' Short title and commencement.- (l) These nrles may be calted the National commission for Backward classes Chairperson' vice-chairperson and Mernbers (conditions of Service and Tenure) Rules. 201g. {21 They shall come into force on the date of their pubtication in the official Cazette. 2. Definitions.- In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,_ (a) "article" means article of the Constitution; (b) "Chairperson', means the Chairperson of the Commission; (c) "commission" means the National commission fbr Backward classes established under clause (l) of article 3l8B; (d) "Member" means a Member of the commission and includes the chairperson and the Vice-chairperson; (e) "Vice-chairperson" means the vice-chairperson of the Commission. 3' Qualilications'- (l) Thc Membcrs shall be appointed from amongst persons of ability, integrity an4 stantling who have had a record ofselfless service to the cause ofjustice for the socially and educationally backward classes. (2) Subject to the provisions of sub rule ( l),_ 4 -- 4 of 6 -- 3(i)l elrffr fr] {InitDi : 3TFITs.IRrrt (a) the chairperson shali be appointed fiom amonsst eminent socio-political ivorkers belongin_e to the sociallv and educationallv backrvard classes' who inspire contldence amongst the sociallv and educationallv bxckiys.d classes by their very personarity and record ofseltress service; (b) ol the vice-chairperson and all other Mcmbers out ,l *'hom. at least two shall be appointed tiom amon-qst persons beronging to the sociaily ancl educationaily backward crasses; (c) ar reast one other Member sha, be appointed rrom amongst w.omeni {' Appointment of part-time lvlembers.- The Members to be appointed under these rules may be appointed on part-time basis also. 5' Term of office'- (l) Save as otherwise provided in these rules, every Member shall hold office fbr a term ol three years irom the tlate on which the Member urrurn", such office. (2) The Nlembers sha, not be erigibre for appointment ror more than two terms. 6' Salaries and allowances'- (l) Every Mlember shall have the rank of a secretary to the Govemment of India unless othenvise specifi ecl. flr:ffl.lJ:T,o"Hlt" be entitled to such salrries, allorvances and orher rhcirities as aclmissibre ro a Secretarv to rhe provided that the chairperson shail also be entitred to a rent ftee accommodation, (3) Notrvithstanding anything contained in sub-rules (r) and (2), if the chairperson, vice-Chairperson or any other Member is a Nlember of Parliament, or a State Legislature, the chairferson, vice-chairperson, or as the case may be, the other lVlember' shall not be entitled to uny ,.runJration other than the alrowances. defined in crause (a) of section 2 of the Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959 (10 of 1959) or as the case may be, other than the allowances, if any, which a Member of the Legislature of the State may, un<ier any law lor the time bein-q a tbrce in the Stare relating ::.J5,JJ;::lion ol disqualification for membership ol the state Legisrature receive without incurring such (1) Notwithstanding anything containetl in sub-rules (l) antl (2), a Member appointed on part-time basis shalt be entitled to t,aveling allo*ance and daily allorvance during tour applicabre to a Secretary to the covernment oi,rndia. 7' Arrangement in case of permanent or temporary vacancies.- (l) Ilthe olllce of the chairperson becomes vacant or if the Chairperson is lor any reason absent or unable to discharge the duties of his otlce, those duties shall, until the new Chairperson asstlmes office or the existing Chairperson res,n.les his otfice. as the case may be, be discharged b1 the Vice-Chairperson. (2) If the otllce of the Vice-chairperson becomes vacant or if the vice-Chairperson is tbr any reason absent orunable to discharge the d*ties of his ofuce, these dr.rties shall. until the new vice-chairperson assumes off.ice or the existing vice-Chairperson resumes his office, be discharged by such other Mernber as presiclent may rJirect. 8' Special provision for retired persons appointed as chairperson, vice-chairperson and other Members of the commission'- where any person' being a retirecl judge of Supreme court or of a High Corrt or a retired government servant or retired servant of any other instirution or autonomous body and in receipt of a pension in respect of any previotts service, is appointed as chairperson, vice-chairperson or a Member, the salary admissible to him under these rules shall be reduced by the amoltnt of that pension and if he had received in lieu of a portion of the pension, the commuted value thereof, by the amount of that portion of the pension, 9' Resignation and Removal'- (l) The chairperson and Vice-Chairperson and any other l\{ember, may, by notice in wnting under his han<I addressetl to president, resign his post. f,., -- 5 of 6 -- \ 6 THE GAZETTE oF INDIA, EXTRAORDINARY fiflff:::I;": the Supreme court, on rei 'rre vrrrLE uy oroer of President on the ground olmisbehuvior aflc-r prescribed by it under ruo.'tttntt being rnade to it by President, has on inquirv heltl in accordance with the procedure .clause (i) of clause (l) of article 145 of the Conrtin,ti on any such ground to be rernoved. ltre Lulrstlnltlon' reported that the Chairperson o.uht (b) presidcnt may suspend frorn ofllce tl supreme courr under this sub-rure untir the..J;iilff*j;::, "::^:::::-ference has bccn matr*o the such reference. ---" Hgrrlu vrusrs o.l recetpt of the report of the Supreme court on ::",.r..r::,lithstanding anvthing in clause (a)' the Presidenr may by order renrove from office the chaiqperson. if the (i) is adjudged an insolvent; (3) The President shall remove a person from the office of vice-chairperson or Member, if that person,- (at becomes an un<lischargetl insolvent; fl,u, ,,fi:;nvicted ancl sentenced to imprisonment for an oflbnce which in the opinion of the presidenr invohes (c) is' in the opinion olthe Presitlent, unfit to conrinue in office by reason of infirmity of mind or body; (d) r.efuses to act or beconres incapable ofacting; :tir.,..t:r:::out obtaining leave of absence from rhe commission, absent tiom three consecurive meetings of the (f) in the opinion of the President, has so abused the position of vice-chairpersoo or Member as to render that persons continuance in office is detrimental to the interest ofthe socially and educationally backward crasses: provided that no person sha' be removed unaer this s*b-rule untir he hi being heard in the matter. ruu-Iure unlll he has been given reasonable opportunity of IF.No. l20l 5, t t i20t7 -BCJI(Vot.
